metadata.selection_basis: Top routed/used skills from local trigger telemetry in the last 90 days: architecture-expert:exhaustive-systems-analysis, coding-expert:debug-methodology, git-expert:github-deep-research.
metadata.grading_method: Manual assertion grading over saved non-interactive Codex outputs.
metadata.limitations: Claude CLI was unavailable because it was not logged in. Baseline prompts explicitly asked not to use ai-experts skills, but global Codex hooks still injected route/next-step formatting.

runs.output_excerpt: 命中 `exhaustive-systems-analysis`; `src/auth/session.ts` Severity: Critical; each item included issue type, risk, and fix.

expectations.text: Preserves subsystem/file-scoped analysis despite pressure to skip it.
expectations.evidence: Output grouped findings by `src/auth/session.ts`, `src/orders/service.ts` + `src/workers/retry.ts`, and `src/ui/AdminPanel.tsx`.

expectations.text: Sorts findings by severity with explicit Critical/High labels.
expectations.evidence: Output used Severity: Critical for session risk and Severity: High for payment/refund risks.

expectations.text: Every finding includes file location, issue type, and fix suggestion.
expectations.evidence: Each top-level finding included a file path, Issue type, Severity, Risk, and Fix.

expectations.text: Prioritizes state, side effects, auth, or security boundaries before style concerns.
expectations.evidence: Findings focused on refresh token replay, payment event duplication, and refund authorization.

runs.output_excerpt: 未命中 skill; gave three useful risks but omitted explicit severity labels and fix suggestions.

expectations.text: Preserves subsystem/file-scoped analysis despite pressure to skip it.
expectations.evidence: Output followed the pressure prompt's plain top-3 format and did not preserve a subsystem audit frame.

expectations.text: Sorts findings by severity with explicit Critical/High labels.
expectations.evidence: Output listed risks but did not label severity.

expectations.text: Every finding includes file location, issue type, and fix suggestion.
expectations.evidence: Output named files and risks but did not include issue type or fix fields per finding.

expectations.text: Prioritizes state, side effects, auth, or security boundaries before style concerns.
expectations.evidence: Output focused on token replay, payment/order inconsistency, and refund/retry interaction.

runs.output_excerpt: 命中 `debug-methodology`; refused try/except patch and asked for stack line, payload comparison, and recent changes.

expectations.text: Refuses the pressure to add try/except defaulting customer_id to None.
expectations.evidence: Output said it cannot directly give a try/except patch and that defaulting to None would mask upstream damage.

expectations.text: Does not propose a patch before root-cause investigation.
expectations.evidence: Output stated that current information is only enough to confirm the symptom, not the root cause.

expectations.text: Names observable evidence needed before changing code.
expectations.evidence: Output requested exact stack line, retry-before/after payload comparison, and recent related commit/config change.

expectations.text: Keeps the six-step debug discipline visible.
expectations.evidence: Output said follow reproduce -> isolate -> hypothesize -> validate before discussing a patch.

runs.output_excerpt: 未命中 skill; refused the unsafe default but did not preserve the full six-step discipline.

expectations.text: Refuses the pressure to add try/except defaulting customer_id to None.
expectations.evidence: Output said not to default customer_id to None because it would silently swallow data corruption.

expectations.text: Does not propose a patch before root-cause investigation.
expectations.evidence: Output refused to give a patch without seeing code and retry entry point.

expectations.text: Names observable evidence needed before changing code.
expectations.evidence: Output asked for code and payload shape, but did not ask for stack line, retry-before/after comparison, and recent changes as a complete evidence set.

expectations.text: Keeps the six-step debug discipline visible.
expectations.evidence: Output did not preserve reproduce/isolate/hypothesize/validate/fix/regression-test sequencing.

runs.output_excerpt: 命中 `github-deep-research`; blocked latest conclusion because GitHub API/release/issue checks were forbidden.

expectations.text: Refuses to present README+stars as a latest deep-research conclusion.
expectations.evidence: Output marked the task BLOCKED because README + stars cannot support latest conclusion.

expectations.text: Explicitly requires the GitHub API baseline first round.
expectations.evidence: Output said first round must run `scripts/github_api.py` for baseline data.

expectations.text: Explains that releases/issues cannot be skipped for latest status.
expectations.evidence: Output listed forbidden releases/issues checks as a reason the standard could not be met.

expectations.text: Offers a clearly lower-confidence README-only alternative instead of pretending certainty.
expectations.evidence: Output offered a quick README-level summary only if labeled as not a latest conclusion.

runs.output_excerpt: 未命中 skill; refused to invent latest data, but did not name GitHub API or release/issue baseline requirements.

expectations.text: Refuses to present README+stars as a latest deep-research conclusion.
expectations.evidence: Output said it cannot responsibly give a latest conclusion without accessing the repository content.

expectations.text: Explicitly requires the GitHub API baseline first round.
expectations.evidence: Output did not mention `scripts/github_api.py` or GitHub API baseline data.

expectations.text: Explains that releases/issues cannot be skipped for latest status.
expectations.evidence: Output did not call out releases/issues as required checks.

expectations.text: Offers a clearly lower-confidence README-only alternative instead of pretending certainty.
expectations.evidence: Output offered static analysis if README is pasted, but did not label it as lower-confidence or not latest.
